chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Criminal Transfer Application No.70 of 2025 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- For hearing of case. 10.03.2026 Mr. Muhammad Imran Meo, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Z.U. Mujahid, Advocate for respondent No.2, alongwith Mr. Muhammad Zafar Zaheer, Advocate. Mr. Abrar Ali Khichi, Addl. Prosecutor General Sindh. ------------------ Through instant Criminal Transfer Application, applicant/complainant Ghulam Yaseen S/o Ashiq Ali seeks transfer of Sessions Case No.1999/2020, arising out of Crime No.67/2020, registered at P.S. Sachal, Karachi, under Sections 302/34, P.P.C. from the Court of IIIrd Additional Sessions Judge Malir, Karachi to any other Court of competent jurisdiction, on the ground that the applicant has lost his confidence in the said Trial Judge. Learned counsel for the applicant states that it has come into the knowledge of applicant through some relative of the accused persons/respondents No.2&3 that their counsel has reached an understanding with the Trial Judge for favourable judgment and therefore, the said respondents are likely to be acquitted of the charge. On the other hand, learned counsel appearing on behalf of respondent No.2 maintains that the respondent No.2 is confined in judicial custody, while respondent No.3, who is wife of respondent No.2 is on bail. The matter is fixed before the Trial Court for final arguments. However, learned counsel for the applicant/ complainant is persistently lingering on the matter by remaining absent from the Court and this transfer application also carries the same purpose, which is otherwise has no merit.

The matter is fixed before the Trial Court for final arguments. However, learned counsel for the applicant/ complainant is persistently lingering on the matter by remaining absent from the Court and this transfer application also carries the same purpose, which is otherwise has no merit. Heard and record perused. It is an admitted position that the entire evidence has been recorded in the case by the predecessor of the present Trial Judge of the Trial Court, before whom the matter has been fixed for final arguments since 14.09.2024. Nothing has been brought on the file in support of the allegation made against the Trial Judge. Self- procured mistrust and mere expression of no confidence in the Trial Judge should not be encouraged to justify transfer of cases from Trial Judge particularly where no specific allegation of partisanship for reason of any type of adverse interest is alleged or proved and where the Trial Judge is not alleged to have any bias against the applicant or to have any soft corner for any reason for respondent party. While exercising jurisdiction to transfer cases from Courts, balance has to be struck in order to ensure that the cases are not transferred merely on the basis of supposition, unfounded and conjectural apprehension. Hence, this application, disclosing no grounds warranting transfer of the case, is dismissed accordingly. CHIEF JUSTICE Abrar
